role: Physician; Personal Doctor to Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
bio: Dr. Seyed Ali Marandi is a prominent Iranian physician who has served as the personal doctor of Supreme Leader [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei). A specialist in pediatrics and nutrition, he served as Minister of Health of Iran from 1989 to 1993 under President [Ali Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali-Akbar_Hashemi_Rafsanjani) and has remained a trusted figure within the conservative establishment. His long-standing personal access to Khamenei as his physician has given the Marandi family exceptional proximity to the regime's inner circle. He is the father of [Mohammad Marandi](/people/mohammad-marandi), the University of Tehran professor, literary critic, and prominent English-language defender of the Islamic Republic, whose regime access is substantially explained by this family connection.
editorial_notes: Father of Mohammad Marandi (in DB). His role as Khamenei's personal physician is the key family tie explaining Mohammad Marandi's regime access. No intelligence or military role — purely physician and former minister.
